title: NestedWGCNA package article
text: NestedWGCNA R package two-stage gene co-expression analysis separates broad biological programs finer, within-program states. Core workflow: Build gene-gene network discover coarse-grained modules (CGMs). Extract CGM core genes apply GenFocus normalization. Re-cluster within CGMs discover fine-grained modules (FGMs). Compared classical WGCNA, NestedWGCNA uses UMAP + HDBSCAN nested decomposition strategy.

title: NestedWGCNA package article
text: package defines explicit method modes: mode = "paper": follows manuscript/README definition. mode = "python_compat": reproduces current Python behavior. mode split main reproducibility contract.

title: NestedWGCNA package article
text: repository R-runtime: core methods implemented R/ Python runtime dependency required case-study scripts Rscript-based Executed real-data case-study scripts:
code: Rscript inst/scripts/run_tcga_blca_case_study.R Rscript inst/scripts/run_all_leukemia_case_study.R Rscript inst/scripts/run_bladderbatch_case_study.R

title: NestedWGCNA package article
text: documentation backed executed analyses three real datasets. Executed real-data datasets
code: summaries <- list(summary_tcga, summary_all, summary_bladder) summaries <- summaries[!vapply(summaries, is.null, logical(1))] if (length(summaries)) {   tbl <- do.call(     rbind,     lapply(       summaries,       function(x) data.frame(         dataset = x$dataset_id,         samples = x$n_samples,         input_genes = x$n_genes_input,         modeled_genes = x$n_genes_model,         target_cgm = x$target_cgm,         cgm_modules = x$cgm_n_modules,         fgm_modules = x$fgm_n_modules,         stringsAsFactors = FALSE       )     )   )   knitr::kable(tbl, caption = "Executed real-data datasets") }

title: NestedWGCNA package article
text: NestedWGCNA provides accessors bundled outputs:
code: available_case_studies() #> [1] "tcga_blca"    "all_leukemia" "bladderbatch" case_study_summary("tcga_blca")$dataset_id #> [1] "tcga_blca" head(case_study_table("all_leukemia", "phenotype_associations")) #>   layer module phenotype  test levels_or_n   effect            p          fdr #> 1   CGM  CGM_5        BT anova           8 60.60704 2.822873e-15 1.242064e-13 #> 2   CGM  CGM_6  mol.biol anova           4 75.23749 1.762997e-10 3.878594e-09 #> 3   CGM  CGM_4        BT anova           8 22.20069 8.971362e-10 1.315800e-08 #> 4   CGM  CGM_5  mol.biol anova           4 50.15517 1.615530e-09 1.777083e-08 #> 5   CGM  CGM_8  mol.biol anova           4 24.02499 3.285927e-08 2.891616e-07 #> 6   CGM  CGM_6        BT anova           8 14.65328 6.453536e-08 4.732593e-07

title: NestedWGCNA package article
text: Following WGCNA eigengene-network visualization conventions, package now provides module-network plotting helpers.
code: tcga_scores <- case_study_table("tcga_blca", "cgm_scores") rownames(tcga_scores) <- tcga_scores[[1]] tcga_scores <- tcga_scores[, -1, drop = FALSE]  tcga_cgm_assign <- case_study_table("tcga_blca", "cgm_assignments") tab <- table(tcga_cgm_assign$cgm[tcga_cgm_assign$cgm >= 0]) cgm_sizes <- stats::setNames(as.numeric(tab), paste0("CGM_", names(tab)))  op <- graphics::par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(1.5, 1.5, 2.8, 1)) plot_module_network(tcga_scores, module_sizes = cgm_sizes, layer = "CGM", top_n = 12, cor_thr = 0.25) plot_module_score_heatmap(tcga_scores, layer = "CGM", top_n = 12) graphics::par(op)

title: NestedWGCNA package article
text: Across datasets, CGMs capture high-level programs FGMs resolve finer substructure within selected CGMs. intended behavior nested design visible three tutorials.

title: Tutorial: ALL leukemia real-data analysis
text: Bioconductor dataset canonical acute lymphoblastic leukemia expression cohort immunophenotype (BT) molecular subtype labels (mol.biol). strong test bed whether nested co-expression modules capture clinically meaningful leukemia structure.

title: Tutorial: ALL leukemia real-data analysis
text: goal test NestedWGCNA modules associated known subtype labels, beyond global gene-expression trends.

title: Tutorial: ALL leukemia real-data analysis
text: tutorial uses real data downloaded Bioconductor package installation: Dataset: ::ExpressionSet Samples: 128 Features (probes): 12,625 Run:
code: system("Rscript inst/scripts/run_all_leukemia_case_study.R")

title: Tutorial: ALL leukemia real-data analysis
text: Pipeline settings run: Select top 1,200 variable probes. CGM discovery min_cluster_size = 30. Attempt GenFocus inside selected CGM; INGS established, use direct FGM clustering CGM matrix. FGM discovery requested min_cluster_size = 8. Association testing module scores BT, mol.biol, sex, age. case-study summary
code: if (!is.null(summary_obj)) {   method_tbl <- data.frame(     item = c(       "samples",       "input probes",       "modeled probes",       "CGM modules",       "FGM modules",       "GenFocus corr_thr",       "GenFocus INGS"     ),     value = c(       summary_obj$n_samples,       summary_obj$n_genes_input,       summary_obj$n_genes_model,       summary_obj$cgm_n_modules,       summary_obj$fgm_n_modules,       val_or_na(summary_obj$genfocus_corr_thr),       summary_obj$genfocus_ings_n     )   )   knitr::kable(method_tbl, caption = "ALL case-study summary") }

code: if (!is.null(summary_obj)) {   res_tbl <- data.frame(     layer = c("CGM", "FGM"),     modules = c(summary_obj$cgm_n_modules, summary_obj$fgm_n_modules),     assigned_genes = c(summary_obj$cgm_assigned_genes, summary_obj$fgm_assigned_genes),     noise_genes = c(summary_obj$cgm_noise_genes, summary_obj$fgm_noise_genes)   )   knitr::kable(res_tbl, caption = "Recovered module structure") } if (!is.null(assoc) && nrow(assoc) > 0) {   top_assoc <- assoc[order(assoc$fdr), c("layer", "module", "phenotype", "test", "effect", "fdr")]   top_assoc <- head(top_assoc, 12)   knitr::kable(top_assoc, digits = 4, caption = "Top phenotype associations") }

title: Tutorial: ALL leukemia real-data analysis
text: Largest CGMs gene count Largest FGMs gene count Association strength layer Top FGM phenotype associations Enrichment interpretation note Main observation run: Strongest statistical signals CGM-level map known labels (BT, mol.biol), best FDR around 1e-13. FGMs still add subtype-linked signal (best FGM FDR around 1e-4), lower magnitude coarse modules. Network topology layered: CGM graph captures broad leukemia structure, FGM graph shows finer within-target relationships.
code: if (!is.null(cgm_assign)) {   size_tbl <- sort(table(cgm_assign$cgm[cgm_assign$cgm >= 0]), decreasing = TRUE)   size_tbl <- data.frame(cgm = names(size_tbl), genes = as.integer(size_tbl))   knitr::kable(head(size_tbl, 7), caption = "Largest CGMs by gene count") } if (!is.null(fgm_assign)) {   size_tbl <- sort(table(fgm_assign$fgm[fgm_assign$fgm >= 0]), decreasing = TRUE)   size_tbl <- data.frame(fgm = names(size_tbl), genes = as.integer(size_tbl))   knitr::kable(head(size_tbl, 7), caption = "Largest FGMs by gene count") } if (!is.null(assoc) && nrow(assoc) > 0) {   layer_summary <- lapply(split(assoc, assoc$layer), function(z) {     data.frame(       layer = unique(z$layer),       tests = nrow(z),       significant_fdr_0_05 = sum(z$fdr < 0.05),       best_fdr = min(z$fdr),       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   })   layer_summary <- do.call(rbind, layer_summary)   knitr::kable(layer_summary, digits = 4, caption = "Association strength by layer") } if (!is.null(assoc) && nrow(assoc) > 0) {   fgm_assoc <- assoc[assoc$layer == "FGM", c("layer", "module", "phenotype", "test", "effect", "fdr")]   fgm_assoc <- fgm_assoc[order(fgm_assoc$fdr), , drop = FALSE]   if (nrow(fgm_assoc)) {     knitr::kable(head(fgm_assoc, 8), digits = 4, caption = "Top FGM phenotype associations")   } } if (is_empty_tbl(cgm_enr) && is_empty_tbl(fgm_enr)) {   knitr::kable(     data.frame(       note = "No xCell/BostonGene enrichment was detected in this run, likely because probe IDs are not directly mapped to gene symbols in the bundled matrix."     ),     caption = "Enrichment interpretation note"   ) }

title: Tutorial: ALL leukemia real-data analysis
text: microarray cohort, known subtype structure dominant, expected CGM-level scores carry effect size. results match expectation: many CGM tests remain significant FDR correction, FGM signals fewer weaker still detectable. network diagram supports reading. CGM modules form clear global backbone corresponding broad subtype organization. FGM modules contribute local edges secondary partitions, indicating nested decomposition redundant even coarse subtype effects strong. Biological enrichment empty run packaged matrix uses probe identifiers included signature collections use gene symbols. annotation-compatibility issue, failure module-discovery step.

title: Tutorial: ALL leukemia real-data analysis
text: practical modeling, use CGM scores primary covariates robust subtype-scale structure, add selected FGMs finer separation needed subgrouping. dataset, objective achieved: stage 1 recovers dominant leukemia axes, stage 2 adds interpretable substructure without replacing coarse signal.

title: Tutorial: bladderbatch real-data analysis
text: cohort small (n = 57) many modeled probes, variance fine-level association testing expected. nested pipeline still provides value: coarse modules identify robust disease-relevant axes, fine modules partition axes follow-exploration. network diagram clarifies behavior. CGM connectivity reveals stable global organization linked phenotype labels, FGM connectivity captures weaker non-random local structure inside selected target module. expected pattern stage 1 high-signal stage 2 power-limited. tutorial, signature enrichment empty expression features probes rather harmonized gene symbols included signature collections.

title: Tutorial: bladderbatch real-data analysis
text: bladderbatch, CGMs interpreted primary disease programs used first inferential analyses. FGMs better treated candidate subprograms validation larger cohorts matched RNA-seq datasets. objective partially achieved: stage 1 yields strong phenotype coupling, stage 2 adds interpretable currently underpowered fine structure.

title: Tutorial: TCGA-BLCA real-data analysis
text: Bulk RNA-seq co-expression analysis often mixes strong tissue-level signals finer immune-state signals. single module scale can miss hierarchy. NestedWGCNA addresses first identifying broad modules (CGMs), re-normalizing re-clustering inside module reveal FGM structure.

title: Tutorial: TCGA-BLCA real-data analysis
text: objective tutorial run fully reproducible, real-data NestedWGCNA workflow TCGA-BLCA interpret whether two-stage design separates broad tumor programs finer immune programs.

title: Tutorial: TCGA-BLCA real-data analysis
text: tutorial uses openly available TCGA-BLCA expression matrix: URL: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ilyada/NestedWGCNA/main/data/TCGA_BLCA.tsv Samples: 406 Genes: 20,017 analysis script downloads expression matrix signature references, runs full R pipeline, writes outputs inst/extdata/case_studies/tcga_blca/.
code: system("Rscript inst/scripts/run_tcga_blca_case_study.R")

title: Tutorial: TCGA-BLCA real-data analysis
text: executed case-study configuration : Log-transform expression keep top 1,200 variable genes. Run CGM discovery (min_cluster_size = 50) find_cgm(). Select immune-like CGM signature enrichment. Attempt GenFocus (focus = "eigengene", corr_thr ladder 0.9 0.7, CVR_thr = 0.6). INGS established, run FGM discovery directly target CGM expression matrix. Run FGM discovery CGM (min_cluster_size = 10). Perform hypergeometric enrichment xCell BostonGene signatures. Case-study analysis settings
code: if (!is.null(summary_obj)) {   method_tbl <- data.frame(     item = c(       "samples",       "input genes",       "modeled genes",       "CGM min_cluster_size",       "FGM min_cluster_size",       "GenFocus corr_thr",       "GenFocus INGS genes"     ),     value = c(       summary_obj$n_samples,       summary_obj$n_genes_input,       summary_obj$n_genes_model,       summary_obj$cgm_min_cluster_size,       summary_obj$fgm_min_cluster_size_used,       val_or_na(summary_obj$genfocus_corr_thr),       summary_obj$genfocus_ings_n     )   )   knitr::kable(method_tbl, caption = "Case-study analysis settings") }

title: Tutorial: TCGA-BLCA real-data analysis
text: Largest CGMs gene count Largest FGMs gene count Observed structure run (goal-aligned findings): CGM-3: strong stromal/mesenchymal program (Astrocytes_FANTOM signatures). CGM-1: epithelial/keratin program (Keratinocytes signatures). CGM-2: macrophage-like immune program (Macrophages_BLUEPRINT signatures). FGM split inside CGM-2: FGM-0: compact MHC-II-like branch (BostonGene MHCII enrichment). FGM-1: macrophage-dominant branch (Macrophages_BLUEPRINT signatures). objective separating coarse tumor context finer immune context met dataset. Co-expression structure first resolves broad stromal/epithelial/immune programs CGM level, resolves immune heterogeneity within target CGM FGM level.
code: if (!is.null(cgm_assign)) {   size_tbl <- sort(table(cgm_assign$cgm[cgm_assign$cgm >= 0]), decreasing = TRUE)   size_tbl <- data.frame(cgm = names(size_tbl), genes = as.integer(size_tbl))   knitr::kable(head(size_tbl, 5), caption = "Largest CGMs by gene count") } if (!is.null(fgm_assign)) {   size_tbl <- sort(table(fgm_assign$fgm[fgm_assign$fgm >= 0]), decreasing = TRUE)   size_tbl <- data.frame(fgm = names(size_tbl), genes = as.integer(size_tbl))   knitr::kable(head(size_tbl, 5), caption = "Largest FGMs by gene count") }

title: Tutorial: TCGA-BLCA real-data analysis
text: two-stage structure supported three independent views. First, module-size distributions show stable coarse partitions focused immune target module. Second, module-score network indicates non-random module-level connectivity rather arbitrary partitions. Third, enrichment shifts broad tissue signatures (CGM) focused immune signatures (FGM). TCGA-BLCA bulk profiles, important stromal immune signals superimposed. one-stage decomposition can blur effects, nested decomposition yields clearer hierarchy.

title: Tutorial: TCGA-BLCA real-data analysis
text: translational analyses, CGM labels can interpreted macro-context modules (stromal-rich, epithelial-rich, immune-rich), FGM labels represent within-context immune states. run, coexistence MHC-II-like branch macrophage-dominant branch inside immune CGM suggests heterogeneity antigen-presentation versus myeloid activity across tumors. layered interpretation directly actionable downstream studies subtype stratification, response association modeling, targeted marker panel design.

title: Tutorial: TCGA-BLCA real-data analysis
text: Results depend variable-gene selection clustering hyperparameters. UMAP + HDBSCAN agreement judged cluster quality biological coherence, label identity alone. Small changes preprocessing can change target CGM selection borderline cases.

code: @Manual{,   title = {NestedWGCNA: Two-Stage Gene Co-Expression Network Analysis},   author = {{Dai}},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://dai540.github.io/NestedWGCNA}, }

title: NestedWGCNA
text: NestedWGCNA R package two-stage gene co-expression network analysis. provides end--end workflow discover coarse-grained modules (CGMs), normalize within selected module cores (GenFocus), discover fine-grained modules (FGMs): https://dai540.github.io/NestedWGCNA/ find_cgm() coarse-grained module discovery genfocus_normalize() core-based normalization find_fgm() fine-grained module discovery selected CGM package intentionally focused. implement raw-count normalization heavy downstream clinical modeling. Instead, standardizes two-stage network workflow, result objects, case-study outputs, reproducible tutorials. designed analysts working expression matrices samples/cells x genes format, especially bulk RNA-seq pseudobulk-style inputs.

title: NestedWGCNA
text: Install GitHub: : install source tarball: load package: Optional dependencies clustering tutorials:
code: install.packages("pak") pak::pak("dai540/NestedWGCNA") install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("dai540/NestedWGCNA") install.packages("path/to/NestedWGCNA_<version>.tar.gz", repos = NULL, type = "source") library(NestedWGCNA) install.packages(c("uwot", "dbscan", "matrixStats")) BiocManager::install(c("ALL", "bladderbatch", "Biobase"))

title: NestedWGCNA
text: use NestedWGCNA, cite package : Dai (2026). NestedWGCNA: Two-Stage Gene Co-Expression Network Analysis. R package. https://dai540.github.io/NestedWGCNA/ can also retrieve citation R:
code: citation("NestedWGCNA")

title: NestedWGCNA
text: https://dai540.github.io/NestedWGCNA/

title: NestedWGCNA
text: NestedWGCNA four things. Computes adjacency/dissimilarity matrices explicit reproducibility modes Discovers CGMs core genes using UMAP + HDBSCAN + core decomposition Runs nested FGM discovery GenFocus-aware fallback behavior Exports standardized case-study tables, enrichment results, figures practice, package : compute_adjacency() compute_dissimilarity() define network space find_cgm() finds CGMs core assignments find_fgm() run_nested_wgcna() run stage-2 decomposition module_score() computes per-sample module scores run_case_study() generates reproducible real-data outputs package supports two main method modes: mode = "paper": adjacency r^2, dissimilarity sqrt(1 - r^2) mode = "python_compat": adjacency |r|, dissimilarity sqrt(1 - |r|)

title: NestedWGCNA
text: run_nested_wgcna() find_cgm() find_fgm() genfocus_normalize() compute_adjacency() compute_dissimilarity() run_case_study() module_score() plot_module_network() plot_module_score_heatmap() available_case_studies() case_study_summary() case_study_table()

title: NestedWGCNA
text: case study generates stable output layout: summary.json cgm_assignments.tsv fgm_assignments.tsv cgm_scores.tsv fgm_scores.tsv cgm_enrichment.tsv fgm_enrichment.tsv phenotype_associations.tsv cgm_module_sizes.png fgm_module_sizes.png

title: NestedWGCNA
code: library(NestedWGCNA)  set.seed(42) expr <- matrix(   abs(rnorm(80 * 500)),   nrow = 80,   ncol = 500,   dimnames = list(paste0("S", seq_len(80)), paste0("G", seq_len(500))) )  res <- run_nested_wgcna(   x = expr,   mode = "paper",   min_cgm_size = 30,   min_fgm_size = 10,   top_n_genes = 400,   seed = 42 )  summary(res)

title: Plot a module-score network — plot_module_network
text: Draw clean module network module-score correlations. function designed NestedWGCNA CGM/FGM score tables follows WGCNA-style eigengene-network visualization principles.

title: Plot a module-score network — plot_module_network
code: plot_module_network(   score_df,   module_sizes = NULL,   layer = "CGM",   top_n = 12,   cor_thr = 0.3,   corr_method = c("spearman", "pearson"),   layout_seed = 42,   show_legend = TRUE )

title: Plot a module-score network — plot_module_network
text: score_df Data frame matrix (samples x modules) module scores. module_sizes Optional named numeric vector module sizes. Names must match colnames(score_df) (example CGM_0, FGM_2). layer Layer label used title. top_n Maximum number modules (highest variance) include. cor_thr Correlation threshold drawing edges. corr_method Correlation method. layout_seed Seed used force-directed layouts. show_legend Whether draw edge-sign legend.

title: Plot a module-score network — plot_module_network
text: Invisibly returns plotted correlation matrix.
